title: Getting Started with Next.js
subtitle: A comprehensive guide to building modern web applications
excerpt: Learn how to set up a Next.js project from scratch and build your first application with best practices.
content:
Next.js has revolutionized the way we build React applications. With its powerful features like server-side rendering, static site generation, and API routes, it provides a complete solution for modern web development.

In this comprehensive guide, we'll walk through setting up a new Next.js project, configuring TypeScript, and building your first application.

$image1

One of the key advantages of Next.js is its file-based routing system. Unlike traditional React applications that require complex routing setups, Next.js automatically creates routes based on the file structure in the pages directory.

$image2

The framework also excels in performance optimization. With features like automatic code splitting, image optimization, and built-in CSS support, your applications will be fast and efficient right out of the box.

image1.caption: The typical folder structure of a Next.js project

image2.caption: How file-based routing works in Next.js

seoTitle: Getting Started with Next.js - Complete Guide
seoDescription: Learn how to set up and build modern web applications with Next.js, React, and TypeScript. Comprehensive guide for beginners.

title: TypeScript Best Practices
subtitle: Writing clean and maintainable TypeScript code
excerpt: Discover essential TypeScript patterns and practices that will make your code more robust and maintainable.
content:
TypeScript has become the de facto standard for large-scale JavaScript applications. Its static typing system helps catch errors early and makes code more self-documenting.

$image1

One of the most important best practices is to use strict mode. This enables all strict type-checking options, providing the highest level of type safety. Always start your TypeScript projects with strict mode enabled.

$image2

Another key practice is to leverage TypeScript's advanced type features like generics, conditional types, and mapped types. These powerful tools can help you create flexible and reusable code patterns.

Remember to use interfaces for object shapes and type aliases for complex type expressions. This separation makes your code more readable and maintainable.
seoTitle: TypeScript Best Practices for Clean Code
seoDescription: Learn essential TypeScript patterns, strict mode configuration, and advanced type features for maintainable code.

title: Responsive Design Principles
subtitle: Creating websites that work on any device
excerpt: Master the fundamental principles of responsive web design to create adaptable and user-friendly interfaces.
content:
Responsive design is no longer optional—it's essential. With the proliferation of mobile devices, your website must provide an excellent experience across all screen sizes.

$image1

The core principles of responsive design include fluid grids, flexible images, and media queries. Fluid grids use relative units like percentages instead of fixed pixels, allowing layouts to adapt to different screen sizes.

$image2

Flexible images scale within their containing elements, preventing them from breaking the layout on smaller screens. Always set max-width: 100% on images to ensure they scale properly.

Media queries are the backbone of responsive design. They allow you to apply different styles based on device characteristics like width, height, and orientation.

image1.caption: How layouts adapt to different screen sizes

seoDescription: Learn fluid grids, flexible images, and media queries to create websites that work perfectly on all devices.

title: Modern JavaScript Features
subtitle: ES6+ features every developer should know
excerpt: Explore the most useful JavaScript features introduced in ES6 and beyond to write cleaner, more efficient code.
content:
JavaScript has evolved tremendously since ES6, introducing features that make the language more powerful and expressive. Understanding these features is crucial for modern web development.

$image1

Arrow functions provide a concise syntax for writing functions and solve the traditional 'this' binding issues. They're perfect for short callback functions and methods.

$image2

Destructuring assignment allows you to extract values from arrays or properties from objects into distinct variables. This makes code more readable and reduces boilerplate.

Template literals provide an elegant way to work with strings, supporting multi-line strings and embedded expressions. They're much more powerful than traditional string concatenation.

image1.caption: Comparison of traditional vs arrow functions

seoDescription: Learn arrow functions, destructuring, template literals, and other essential ES6+ JavaScript features.
